title: Spicy Crispy Egyptian Chicken Stir-fry
description: A keto-friendly and spicy twist on traditional Egyptian cuisine, combining the crunch of fried chicken with the flavors of Asia.
instructions: Cut the chicken into bite-sized pieces and season with salt, cumin, coriander, and black pepper.
Heat 20ml of vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the wok and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the wok and set aside.
Add the remaining 20ml of vegetable oil to the wok and cook until fragrant.
Add the garlic, ginger, and scallions to the wok and cook until softened.
Add the gochujang and sambal oelek to the wok and cook for 1 minute.
Add the Shaoxing wine to the wok and cook until the liquid has almost completely evaporated.
Return the chicken to the wok and stir to coat with the spicy sauce.
Serve the chicken hot, garnished with parsley and additional scallions if desired.
Serve with steamed vegetables or a side of rice for a well-rounded meal.
Optional: Top with crispy fried onions or crushed peanuts for added crunch.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
